{"society_power": 8.0, "state_power": 6.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Mexican War/Texas expansion; reform movements vs. rising Slave Power", "reasoning": "State capacity grew through territorial expansion and military mobilization (Texas annexation, Mexican-American War), lifting state power modestly to 6.5. Society's associational and participatory vigor (abolitionism, Seneca Falls, Free Soil, suffrage expansion) thickened but was offset by the Slave Power's capture of institutions and suppression of Black and dissenting society, leaving net society power roughly steady at 8.0."}
